mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in 60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and elevated recession probability, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, forecasting negative equity returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth demonstrates expanding economic activity and higher corporate profit margins, which systematically supports positive forward returns for broad equity indices.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 60-day forward returns in 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in 55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units during a post-shock recovery phase indicate renewed construction investment and economic stabilization, forecasting positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1500 in recovery regimes are followed by negative 5-day returns in over 50% of cases.
